name: Qatar
summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday): **QATAR STAYED IN THE MEDIATOR LANE AS IRAN STRUCK THREE US-ALLIED CAPITALS — NO DIRECT STRIKE ON QATAR THIS WINDOW; LNG/HELIUM WAR PREMIUM PERSISTS (TTF ~€51/MWh, +11% 1M).** (intel A5, A9) On June 10 the IRGC launched drones/missiles at Ali Al Salem/Kuwait, the Fifth Fleet HQ/Bahrain, and — for the first time — Azraq/Jordan (all intercepted, zero damage per a US official; IRGC '21 targets / 4 destroyed' claim DENIED) — but Qatar was NOT a direct target this window. Doha/Al Jazeera stayed in the mediator lane: 'multiple countries urged both to pull back from the brink.' QATAR COMMODITY CONTEXT (carried, unchanged structurally): ~17% of LNG liquefaction (Ras Laffan Trains 4/6) remains offline (force majeure, repairs 'years'), keeping a war premium in European TTF gas (~€51/MWh, +11% 1M); helium ~30% offline from the Ras Laffan south site, near-term cliff eased (SK hynix 'secured inventory', CLAIMED). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: NO Qatar trigger moves — no direct strike, no second-EEZ-vessel-strike, no mediator-named change; mediation edges unchanged (Qatar was not an active negotiating venue this window, only a rhetorical de-escalation voice). STAY WATCHING. PRIOR — Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session -> 5-6 June). **CARRY-FORWARD — HELIUM/LNG CONTEXT UNCHANGED; EUROPEAN TTF GAS €48.76/MWh (~FLAT 1D, +11% MoM, +33% YoY); RAS LAFFAN ~17% LIQUEFACTION FORCE MAJEURE (REPAIRS 'YEARS').** No new Qatar-specific development this window: European TTF gas held ~flat 1D at €48.76/MWh (+11% MoM, +33% YoY), the Qatar Ras Laffan ~17% liquefaction force majeure remains the structural floor under European LNG pricing (repairs are years out), and the near-term helium cliff stays eased (SK hynix 'secured inventory', CLAIMED). No weight change on the mediation edges; no trigger moves. PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **QATAR REVERSED ITS SILENCE AND CONDEMNED — THE GULF CLOSED RANKS RHETORICALLY (GCC COLLECTIVE CONDEMNATION), BUT ONLY KUWAIT TOOK DIPLOMATIC ACTION.** The key delta vs yesterday (when Qatar had NOT condemned): Qatar issued a STRONG condemnation of the attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain and full solidarity with both (The Peninsula Qatar, intel A3, CONFIRMED) — and the GCC issued a collective condemnation (Sec-Gen Albudaiwi: 'security of Kuwait and Bahrain is inseparable from Gulf security,' calling for 'a firm international response'). Saudi, UAE, Bahrain, Qatar, Jordan, Egypt and Lebanon all expressed solidarity. BUT no Gulf state followed Kuwait's expulsion of two Iranian diplomats — the diplomatic escalation remains Kuwait-only; the rest held the rhetorical-condemnation line without parallel action. The durable signal is the SPLIT between rhetoric and action: collective condemnation, Kuwait-only rupture; Qatar condemned but did not expel and still hosts both Al Udeid and Iran ties. On the commodity side: the near-term helium cliff EASED (SK hynix said it secured inventory, intel A11); TTF flat at EUR48.76/MWh (storage only 38% full, Ras Laffan ~17% force majeure floor intact). No weight change on the mediation edges. PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **QATAR HELD A MEDIATOR-NEUTRAL POSTURE AS A NEIGHBOURING GULF HOST (KUWAIT) TOOK CASUALTIES AND BROKE WITH TEHRAN — A NOTABLE NON-MOVE WHILE THE GULF TILTS IRAN-CRITICAL.** On the 3 June barrage, the lethal damage fell on Kuwait (1 dead, 63 injured) and Kuwait expelled two Iranian diplomats; Bahrain intercepted strikes on its own 'civilian sites.' Qatar (via Al Jazeera) covered the strikes and the Indian death straight but itself stayed in mediator-neutral posture — no condemnation of Iran surfaced, unlike Kuwait's 'criminal Iranian aggression' line. The question for the desk: if the Gulf consensus tips from 'neutral mediator' to 'condemning Iran' (Bahrain/UAE the swing states), Tehran's regional diplomatic room shrinks — Qatar staying neutral is the marker that the tip has NOT yet happened. On the commodity side: TTF gas rose +2.77% to €48.93/MWh (>€49) on the stalled talks + EU storage only ~38% full ahead of winter; the Qatar Ras Laffan ~17% liquefaction force majeure remains the structural floor; the helium clock (Korea fabs buffered 'until June,' now inside the window) and the urea-cascade (CF +2.5%) persist. No weight change on the geopolitical mediation edges. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **THE QATAR LNG PREMIUM RE-PRICED UP ON THE HORMUZ CLOSURE THREAT, BUT THE MEDIATION ECOSYSTEM IS STRAINED — IRAN SUSPENDED THE US TALKS QATAR HELPED FACILITATE, AND A MEDIATOR-HOST (KUWAIT) WAS DIRECTLY STRUCK. On the commodity side, Iran's 1 June Hormuz 'complete closure' threat + the Ras Laffan legacy bid Cheniere +5% and the broader LNG complex (US Gulf Coast pivot) — but European TTF actually FELL -2.85% (the closure is a threat, not an act; the physical curve didn't move). The Qatar Ras Laffan ~17% liquefaction force majeure remains the structural floor (+32% YoY TTF). On mediation, the suspension removes the immediate Iran-US channel Qatar/Oman were facilitating, while the Israel-Lebanon DC political track (4th round, 2-3 June) continues in parallel; the Iranian missile salvo at US forces in Kuwait put a Gulf mediator-ecosystem state directly in the kinetic frame. Net: Qatar is absorbing a direct regional escalation while the diplomatic scaffolding it built just got suspended — a strained, event-overtaken mediation posture. No weight change on the geopolitical edges. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30). **QATAR STAYS MEDIATION-FORWARD — IN THE PENTAGON ISRAEL-LEBANON ECOSYSTEM AND COOL ON THE ACCORDS. The first-ever Pentagon Israel-Lebanon military talks (29 May, hosted by Under Sec. Colby) were called 'productive,' to feed a State-led political track 'next week' — part of the Gulf mediation ecosystem Qatar sits within. On the Accords, Trump's 'mandatory' demand left Gulf capitals (Qatar among those 'stunned') slow-walking; Saudi reaffirmed a Palestinian-state precondition. On the commodity side the Qatar-disruption premium is CRACKING on the fast legs: urea is -35% MoM and Cheniere -17% MoM as deal-anticipation prices in Gulf LNG/fertilizer export normalization — though TTF gas (EUR45.96/MWh, ~+30% YoY floor) and the helium read remain elevated, and the Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure persists.** Day 90 of Hormuz blockade (Thursday May 28). **THREE QATAR THREADS, NO RESOLUTION TODAY. (1) HELIUM — Qatar supplies ~1/3 of global helium and ~1/3 of that capacity is OFFLINE at Ras Laffan; the documented Ras Laffan→helium→semiconductor cascade is DELAYED, NOT ABSENT — Korea's ~6-month stockpiles buffer it, so the cascade re-emerges ~Nov 2026 rather than now. (2) MEDIATION — Qatar's active-mediator role (Doha talks venue) continues; the US is NOT physically at the table. (3) ABRAHAM ACCORDS — Trump told Saudi Arabia and Qatar to 'go first,' yet Qatar remains conspicuously SILENT (no public response) — a coordinated slow-walk, consistent with Pakistan's on-the-record rejection and the Saudi silence. Also note: the day's dominant kinetic event hit KUWAIT, not Qatar — the IRGC fired one calibrated ballistic missile at a US airbase in Kuwait (intercepted, zero casualty) — watch for GCC condemnation. No new Qatar-specific development resolves any of the three threads today.** PRIOR (May 27): **QATAR IS NOW AN INCREASINGLY ACTIVE DEAL-SHUTTLE: it hosted Iran's Ghalibaf/Araghchi/Hemmati delegation in Doha (Qatar's PM the host) on Hormuz + HEU + frozen-asset unfreezing; the delegation has since concluded its visit and returned to Tehran [CONFIRMED ACTION]. Trump says the Hormuz-reopening deal is 'largely negotiated' (draft MOU: 60-day ceasefire extension, Hormuz reopened 'no tolls' + Iran clears mines, US lifts the port blockade + sanctions waivers, HEU 'disposal,' 'strong inspections') but it stays structurally unresolved on its three hardest clauses — Hormuz control, the nuclear file, and whether the ceasefire covers Israel's Hezbollah ops (Iran insists Lebanon is covered).** Separately, Qatar is a named mandatory-Abraham-Accords target told to 'go first,' yet gave NO public response — and per a US official cited by Axios its leader was among those who 'appeared stunned' on the weekend call (REPORTED, single Axios channel); the named-nation list widened May 26 from six to eight (UAE + Bahrain added). Qatar's conspicuous non-amplification is the slow-walk signal [RHETORIC — no acceptance]. The US is NOT physically at the table — Qatar is mediating. PRIOR (May 25): **SENIOR IRANIAN DELEGATION ARRIVED IN DOHA MAY 25 — SPEAKER GHALIBAF, FM ARAGHCHI AND CENTRAL BANK GOVERNOR HEMMATI MET QATAR'S PM TO WORK HORMUZ + HIGHLY-ENRICHED URANIUM + UNFREEZING OF IRANIAN ASSETS. THE PRESENCE OF THE CENTRAL-BANK GOVERNOR CONFIRMS THE FROZEN-ASSET MECHANICS ARE NOW A LIVE TECHNICAL WORKSTREAM, NOT RHETORIC: PER FARS, 'WASHINGTON HAS AGREED TO RELEASE PART OF TEHRAN'S FROZEN OVERSEAS ASSETS' IN EXCHANGE FOR LIFTING HORMUZ RESTRICTIONS / THE NAVAL BLOCKADE.** Qatar's mediator role is now 'increasingly active' — a material upgrade from pure-messenger: yesterday a Qatari team was in Tehran; today Iran's economic and diplomatic principals are in Doha [CONFIRMED ACTION — Euronews, Daily Sabah, Detroit News/AP, Press TV, CNN]. **Crucially the US is NOT physically at the table in Doha — Qatar is shuttling.** Separately, Trump's 'mandatory Abraham Accords' demand (six Muslim nations to 'simultaneously' sign, telling Saudi and Qatar to 'go first') directly targets Doha; per Axios the Gulf/Muslim leaders met it with 'silence on the line' — Qatar's conspicuous non-amplification is the slow-walk signal [RHETORIC — no acceptance]. PRIOR (May 24): **QATARI NEGOTIATING TEAM ARRIVED IN TEHRAN FRIDAY MAY 22 IN COORDINATION WITH THE US — DUAL MEDIATION ARCHITECTURE NOW OPERATIONALLY EXPLICIT WITH PARALLEL DELEGATIONS (PAKISTAN COAS MUNIR + QATARI TEAM IN TEHRAN SIMULTANEOUSLY). QATAR'S EMIR SHEIKH TAMIM HELD CALLS WITH MBS AND MBZ ON REGIONAL DEVELOPMENTS — THE CLEANEST SINGLE GULF-COORDINATION SIGNAL OF THE CYCLE ON THE DEAL TRACK. AL JAZEERA RAN MUNIR-TEHRAN + BAGHAEI '30-60 DAYS' + DUAL-MEDIATION PAKISTAN+QATAR ARCHITECTURE AS LEAD DIPLOMATIC FRAME MAY 23-24.** (1) **Qatari negotiating team in Tehran since Friday May 22** (REPORTED — Reuters via JPost and Investing.com): the delegation arrived 'in coordination with the United States to try to help secure a deal to end the war with Iran and resolve outstanding issues' — pre-existing the Saturday Trump 'largely negotiated' Truth Social post. **The delegation is parallel to Munir's Pakistan-COAS visit** — both in Tehran simultaneously, suggesting the deal architecture deliberately bifurcates the political-public track (Pakistan/COAS-military) from the substantive-text track (Qatar/IRGC-backchannel). (2) **Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani held calls with MbS and MbZ on regional developments** (CONFIRMED — Türkiye Today, Yeni Safak). This **operationally activates GCC alignment around the MOU at the Doha-centred-leadership level**: Doha is positioned at the centre of GCC alignment around the deal architecture. (3) **Al Jazeera (Qatar state-influenced) lead coverage May 23-24** ran Munir-Tehran + Baghaei '30-60 days' + dual-mediation Pakistan+Qatar as lead diplomatic frame — Qatari editorial channel positions Pakistan as formal mediator while Qatar runs substantive backchannel. The choice of Al Arabiya (Saudi) for the May 21-22 deal-text leak rather than Al Jazeera = MbS positioning for primary deal-credit; Qatar's mediation-architecture role is more durable than headline-credit. (4) **TTF Gas €48.69/MWh May 22 close** (-1.46% 1D, +2.1% MoM, +30%+ vs ~€31 baseline) — flat-to-easing; Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure 17% liquefaction persists but Norway maintenance ease holding. **No European institutional escalation through May 24** on the Al Jazeera May 22 single-source 'EU moves to sanction Iran over Hormuz blockade' piece — still NOT corroborated by Reuters Europe / Politico Europe / Euractiv / Le Monde primary sourcing. Treat as REPORTED LOW-CONFIDENCE pending EU Council or member-state primary statement. (5) **Qatar's structural role at maximum operational expression**: deal-text leak channel = Al Arabiya/Saudi; spoiler-framing protection = Al Jazeera/Qatar; substantive backchannel via IRGC generals + parallel Qatari negotiating team in Tehran. **The Qatari Emir's Saturday phone-call architecture (MbS + MbZ) is the cleanest single Gulf-coordination signal of the cycle on the deal track.** (6) **Helium clock**: TrendForce's March 24 'two-week clock' implies Asian chip fab inventory exhaustion at full production rates was passed without confirmed Samsung/SK hynix production halt; Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final qualification → June supply ramp positioned. Korean fabs maintaining production via diversified suppliers + recycling + reduced output. **Kornbluth helium thesis remains materially constrained** by mitigation. PRIOR (May 23): AL JAZEERA (QATAR) FRAMED MUNIR-TEHRAN VISIT AS LEAD DIPLOMATIC STORY MAY 22-23 — IRANIAN OFFICIAL QUOTED BY AL JAZEERA DESCRIBED QATAR'S ROLE AS 'VITAL, IMPORTANT, AND FUNDAMENTAL TO SUPPORTING PAKISTAN'S MEDIATION.' DUAL-CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (PAKISTAN FORMAL/MILITARY + QATAR SUBSTANTIVE/IRGC BACKCHANNEL) NOW OPERATIONALLY EXPLICIT.** Al Jazeera continued 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire' framing into May 23 — protecting Qatari mediation space while channeling deal-spoiler narrative onto Israel. **TTF Gas €48.69/MWh May 22 close** (-1.46% 1D, +2.1% MoM, +30%+ vs ~€31 baseline) — flat-to-easing; Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure 17% liquefaction persists but Norway maintenance ease holding. **Helium clock**: TrendForce's March 24 'two-week clock' implies ~early-April Asian chip fab inventory exhaustion at full production rates was already PASSED without confirmed Samsung/SK hynix production halt; Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final qualification → June supply ramp positioned. Korean fabs maintaining production via diversified suppliers + recycling + reduced output. **Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan publicly praised Trump diplomacy + Pakistan mediation** (Al Arabiya May 22-23) — Gulf editorial coordination intact with Saudi taking deal-credit position, Qatar taking mediation-architecture position, UAE restraint, Oman quiet backchannel. **The choice of Al Arabiya rather than Al Jazeera for the May 21-22 leak is intelligence — MbS positioning Saudi as primary deal-credit recipient even though Qatar is operational backchannel** (per May 10 Axios scoop on Qatar's IRGC-side contacts). **Qatar's structural role at maximum operational expression**: deal-text leak channel = Al Arabiya/Saudi; spoiler-framing protection = Al Jazeera/Qatar; substantive backchannel via IRGC generals continues. PRIOR (May 22): **AL JAZEERA (QATAR) FRAMED ISRAEL AS DEAL-SPOILER MAY 21 — 'ISRAEL PUSHES FOR WAR AMID US CEASEFIRE, BUT ITS OPTIONS MAY BE LIMITED.' QATARI EDITORIAL ALIGNMENT OPENLY FRAMING ISRAEL AS THE THREAT TO THE PAKISTAN-MEDIATED DEAL — PROTECTS QATARI ROLE AS OPERATIONAL BACKCHANNEL WHILE CHANNELING DEAL-SPOILER NARRATIVE ONTO ISRAEL.** Al Arabiya (Saudi) leaked the Pakistan-mediated 'final draft' text May 21-22 — **the choice of Al Arabiya rather than Al Jazeera for the leak is intelligence**: MbS is positioning Saudi Arabia as primary deal-credit recipient even though Qatar is the operational backchannel (per May 10 Axios scoop on Qatar's Iran-side contacts via senior IRGC generals). **Three-Gulf-leader editorial coordination operational through three different channels**: Al Arabiya (deal-text leak — Saudi); Al Jazeera (Israel-as-spoiler frame — Qatar); The National (restraint — UAE). **TTF Gas €47.69/MWh May 22** (vs €50.17 May 19, -2% retracement; -3.48% 1D / +7.38% 1M / +30.7% YoY): shallow retracement despite Brent -5.3% — European energy market pricing nuclear-track unresolved + Qatar Ras Laffan 17% force majeure as structural, not contingent on the leaked text. **Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery** — structural floor under European LNG pricing remains Qatar-specific. **Helium clock Day 7 of 14** — Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final tests with June supply ramp (CONFIRMED — Digitimes / Trendforce); SK Hynix Q1 call disclosed 'sufficient helium inventory' + diversified suppliers (CNBC April 23). **Kornbluth helium thesis materially constrained** by mitigation; 60-day production-cut path now the LESS LIKELY branch. **Qatar's structural role in the deal architecture is operationally maximal**: Al Jazeera deal-spoiler framing protects Qatari role; substantive backchannel via IRGC generals continues per Axios May 10 framing; Ras Laffan LNG outage continues to provide European-pricing structural floor. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **A2 NAQVI-PEZESHKIAN 90-MIN PRIVATE MEETING SUNDAY MAY 17 — PAKISTAN-NAQVI MEDIATION DAY 2 OF 14 FRAMEWORK-DELIVERABLE TRIGGER WINDOW.** Qatar's structural role unchanged: Qatari PM al-Thani back-channel architecture continues to be the substantive Iran-side channel via IRGC senior generals (Axios May 10 framing carries forward). **Al Jazeera (Qatar state-influenced) continues 'ceasefire in name only' scare-quoted framing on Israel-Lebanon while staying restrained on the Saudi drone story** — Qatar positioning to remain the mediator, not the prosecutor. **TTF Gas €50.16/MWh Monday — flat versus Friday €50.85 close** (within daily noise); past month +18.26%; +42.75% YoY; ~20% of global LNG supply disrupted since war began. Friday's +6.7% one-day move NOT extending Monday — gas complex treating maximal-pressure weekend tape as already priced. **The TTF tape is the cleanest 'not capitulating yet' signal in the European cascade complex** — paired with VIX sub-22 (18.43) + KOSPI rally, vol-pricing structure not gapping despite kinetic preparation. **Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery; the structural floor under European LNG pricing remains Qatar-specific.** Helium Day 3 of 14 TrendForce clock; Korean 6-mo buffer ~June-July; 30-38% global output offline. **PRIOR (May 16): Day 78. SECOND STRIKE INSIDE QATARI EEZ TRIGGER — Day 6 of 7 since May 10 Safesea Neha — NO 2ND STRIKE through May 16 morning** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO operational primary). The Doha bulk-carrier-strike story has now passed Day 6 of the 7-day window with **NO Qatar MoFA statement — Qatar institutionally absorbing the strike rather than escalating, consistent with the substantive-mediator posture**. **A9 HELIUM-CLOCK-EXPIRY WINDOW MAY 16-23 BEGINS TODAY** — Korea sources 64.7% of helium from Qatar; KOSPI -6.1% confirmed close 7,493.18 is the price-discovery anchor. **B4 TTF GAS €49.86/MWh May 15 (+4.63% 1D, third consecutive daily increase, highest since April; pre-war Europe sourced 12-14% of LNG from Qatar)** — driver chain ongoing Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure on ~17% of liquefaction capacity. **Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa) still 3-5yr recovery; the structural floor under European LNG pricing remains Qatar-specific.** Qatari PM Mohammed bin Abdulrahman al-Thani back-channel architecture continues to be the substantive Iran-side channel via IRGC senior generals (Axios May 10 framing carries forward). Al Jazeera (Qatari state-aligned) carried both the Saudi-Helsinki-Accords-pact story and the Hormuz Chinese-vessels-transit story — Qatari mediator-positioning consistent. PRIOR (May 15): **A11 SECOND STRIKE INSIDE QATARI EEZ TRIGGER — Day 5 of 7 since May 10 Safesea Neha bulk-carrier strike — NO 2ND STRIKE through May 15 morning** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO operational primary). The Doha bulk-carrier-strike story has now passed Day 5 of the 7-day window with **NO Qatar MoFA statement — Qatar institutionally absorbing the strike rather than escalating, consistent with the substantive-mediator posture**. **A9 HELIUM 2-WEEK-CLOCK NOW AT DAY 74 SINCE RAS LAFFAN HALT — HELIUM-CLOCK-EXPIRY WINDOW MAY 16-23 BEGINS TOMORROW**. Korea sources 64.7% of helium from Qatar (2025); KOSPI -6.1% today is the price-discovery confirmation that supply-side stack is no longer in 'no priced risk' mode. **B7 TTF GAS €47.61/MWh May 14 (+1.47% 1D, +15% 1M, +34.65% YoY)** — creeping up, second consecutive daily increase, but remains well below Goldman-estimated tail scenario ~€72/MWh. **Xi at Trump-Xi joint statement opposed 'any effort to charge a toll' for Hormuz use — direct rhetorical strike at Mokhber's 'atomic-bomb' doctrine; China is interested in purchasing more American oil to reduce dependence on the Strait** = potential structural channel that bypasses Hormuz altogether benefits Qatar LNG pricing-power preservation. **Trump-Xi joint statement reduces tail-risk on rhetoric; physical Qatar LNG outage continues to provide structural floor.** Al Jazeera (Qatari state-aligned) carried the Israel-Lebanon Washington talks with Lebanese-perspective framing (512 Lebanese killed since April 17). Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery; Qatar formally named primary mediator architecture (Pakistan official + Qatar substantive + Saudi underwriter) remains intact. **A3/A8 SECOND STRIKE INSIDE QATARI EEZ TRIGGER — Day 4 of 7 since May 10 Safesea Neha bulk-carrier strike — NO 2ND STRIKE through May 14 morning** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO operational primary). The Doha bulk-carrier-strike story has now passed Day 4 of the 7-day window with **NO Qatar MoFA statement — Qatar institutionally absorbing the strike rather than escalating, consistent with the substantive-mediator posture**. Al Jazeera (Qatari state-aligned) carried Wang Yi-Iran framing favourably May 14, consistent with Qatar's mediator positioning and the Qatar-China alignment that today's BRICS-FM Delhi setting reinforces. **B4 TTF GAS €46.63 MAY 13 (basically flat, -0.11% d/d)** — at the €46 range yesterday's brief noted (€46.23 May 11); no fresh European Council action; Cheniere CCO Hormuz disruption framing carries forward; container freight FBX13 (China/East Asia → Mediterranean) at $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) carries forward. **Helium 2-week-clock at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2 = TIME-SENSITIVE EXHAUSTION WINDOW MAY 16-23 (per markets dossier section E proposed trigger).** Qatar Ras Laffan production-halt remains the single most consequential supply-chain pivot in the cycle — 30% of global helium routed through it. PRIOR (May 13): **A3 SECOND COMMERCIAL VESSEL STRIKE INSIDE QATARI EEZ TRIGGER (May 10 + 7 days) — NOT TRIPPED through May 12 Day 2 of 7 sample-size 1, pattern undeclared. VESSEL ATTRIBUTION OF MAY 10 STRIKE CLARIFIED: Marshall Islands-flagged Safesea Neha, US-managed by NJ-based Safesea Group (logistics for UN peacekeeping, WFP, GSA) — strike on US-managed vessel during peace talks materially more politically loaded than May 10 unflagged framing. 2nd Safesea vessel hit cycle (Safesea Vishnu, March 12 STS Iraqi waters, killed one Indian crew). NO party has claimed.** **A12 SAUDI FM TRILATERAL MAY 11: Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan separately called Iranian FM Araghchi and Pakistani FM Ishaq Dar to discuss 'Pakistan's mediation efforts.'** Pakistan formally still official mediator; Qatar substantive (consistent with May 10 Axios scoop). Multi-channel architecture (Pakistan official + Qatar substantive + Saudi underwriter + Russia uranium-custodian standing offer) is publicly visible. **The trigger 'Iran responds via Qatar-IRGC channel rather than Pakistan-FM channel within 7 days' (added May 10) is at Day 2 of 7 — IRAN'S FORMAL MAY 10-11 RESPONSE WENT VIA PAKISTAN PER PUBLIC REPORTING (no Qatar-IRGC channel attribution); substantive-mediator-via-Qatar trigger has NOT been confirmed by operational signal yet.** Qatari and Saudi MoFAs remained publicly silent on the Doha bulk carrier strike (Day 2 of 7-day next-strike trigger). **Saudi visibly distancing from UAE's military track in light of WSJ Lavan scoop (B6).** HELIUM/LNG/FERTILIZER TRIPLE-CHOKEPOINT INTENSIFYING: Korean fab buffer ~4 weeks remaining as of May 12 (mid-June exhaustion). TTF €46.23 May 11 (+4.74% d/d, +5% from May 8 €44 baseline). Urea US Gulf FOB +50% since war began (Discovery Alert/Argus); UBS raised CF target $97→$140 explicitly noting Hormuz disruption. India DAP tender (IPL May 11) lowest offers $935/t cfr east coast / $930/t cfr west coast (vs $580-650 ex-crisis). Modi Hyderabad address May 10-11 (cut fertilizer 50%) reads as Indian-government public confirmation of Qatar Ras Laffan offline as binding shock, not hypothetical. The 'Qatar formally named primary mediator within 14 days' April 29 trigger is at Day 13 of 14 — multi-channel architecture has solidified into formal pattern; Qatar SUBSTANTIVE via IRGC senior generals but Pakistan retains OFFICIAL title. Qatari PM Mohammed bin Abdulrahman al-Thani met VP JD Vance in Washington Friday May 8, then changed planned return to Doha and instead flew to Miami Saturday May 9 to meet Sec. Rubio and special envoy Witkoff (CONFIRMED — Axios primary scoop by Barak Ravid; Times of Israel, Newsmax, Israel National News, TASS, Tribune India). Per Axios's source: 'While Pakistan has been the official mediator between the U.S. and Iran since the beginning of the war, the Qataris have been working behind the scenes... White House views them as especially effective in negotiations with Iran.' Critically: 'the Qataris are functioning as one of at least three back channels between the U.S. and Iran' and 'are using their contacts with senior Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps generals involved in Iran's decision-making.' First publicly-reported acknowledgment of: (a) at least three active back channels, (b) Qatar as the most-trusted Iran-side channel from WH perspective, (c) IRGC senior generals as Iran-side counterparties — NOT the FM channel. **Structural read:** Pakistan retains the OFFICIAL mediator title and parliamentary/spokesperson visibility, but the SUBSTANTIVE channel from the US side now appears to flow through Qatar to the IRGC. Political mediation (Pakistan) and operational mediation (Qatar→IRGC) now run on two parallel tracks possibly with different counterparties. **B1 BULK CARRIER STRUCK BY UNKNOWN PROJECTILE 23NM NE OF DOHA (~43KM) SUNDAY MORNING (NEW — MAJOR OPERATIONAL):** First commercial vessel attack since CMA CGM San Antonio (May 5) and FIRST near Qatar's coast — well inside Qatar's EEZ, immediately offshore from Hamad Port and Ras Laffan, ordinarily considered the safest waters in the Persian Gulf. Small fire extinguished by crew, no casualties; UKMTO Warning notice posted; vessel flag/owner not publicly disclosed. NO CLAIM OF RESPONSIBILITY — IRGC has not claimed (consistent with May 5 CMA CGM 'claim-management discrimination pattern'). The geographic significance is acute: location is approximately equidistant from Iran's Bushehr coast and Qatar's main port complex. **Three reading frames operationally consistent:** (i) IRGC signal to Qatar that mediator role does not buy immunity for blockade-aligned shipping — concurrent with al-Thani Miami meeting; (ii) Houthi-style proxy operation (LOW probability — wrong geometry); (iii) misfire/accidental engagement (possible). No Saudi MoFA or Qatar MoFA statement at filing time. **HELIUM/LNG/FERTILIZER TRIPLE-CHOKEPOINT UNCHANGED:** Korean fab buffer 6-12 weeks per Smith/Discovery Alert = ~5 weeks remaining as of May 10 (mid-June exhaustion). Cheniere CCO 'astounded prices haven't risen higher with closure disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' TTF ~€44/MWh (-25%+ from March peak, +40% from pre-war ~€31). Urea Yuzhny FOB $616/mt May 6; DAP NCIC tender $880/mt fob (vs $840 Apr 4). The 'Qatar formally named primary mediator within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)' April 29 trigger is at Day 11 of 14 — re-evaluation is now warranted: Qatar is NOT formally named in public-facing architecture (Pakistan retains OFFICIAL title) but IS the substantive Iran-side channel via IRGC generals. The trigger should be reframed to capture multi-channel structure rather than a single-mediator-named test.

current.mediation_posture: June 10: no direct strike on Qatar; Doha/Al Jazeera stayed in the mediator lane ('multiple countries urged both to pull back from the brink') as Iran struck Kuwait/Bahrain/Jordan (intercepted, zero damage).

Qatar attended the Bürgenstock signing as co-mediator/guarantor AND co-brokered (with the US) the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire that took effect 4pm 19 Jun — a double mediation success. Doha's role institutionalised across both the US-Iran deal and the Lebanon truce. CONFIRMED.

Qatar's mediator role reaffirmed — Doha to host separate pre-signing meetings with each side this week ahead of the 19 Jun Switzerland ceremony (alongside Pakistani mediation). Gulf states unambiguously pro-deal given the commercial stake in a Hormuz reopening. REPORTED.

**QATAR NOT STRUCK — STAYED IN THE MEDIATOR LANE AS IRAN HIT KUWAIT/BAHRAIN/JORDAN; LNG/HELIUM WAR PREMIUM PERSISTS (TTF ~€51/MWh, +11% 1M).** On June 10 the IRGC launched drones/missiles at Ali Al Salem/Kuwait, the Fifth Fleet HQ/Bahrain, and — for the first time — Azraq airbase/Jordan (all intercepted, zero damage per a US official; IRGC '21 targets / 4 destroyed' claim DENIED) — but Qatar was NOT a direct target this window. Commodity context (carried, unchanged structurally): ~17% of Qatar LNG liquefaction (Ras Laffan Trains 4/6) remains offline (force majeure, repairs 'years'), keeping a war premium in European TTF gas (~€51/MWh, +11% 1M); helium ~30% offline from Ras Laffan's south site, near-term cliff eased (SK hynix 'secured inventory', CLAIMED). No weight change on mediation edges; no trigger moves.

recent_signals.headline: **THREE QATAR THREADS, NONE RESOLVED TODAY: (1) HELIUM CASCADE IS DELAYED, NOT ABSENT — ~1/3 OF GLOBAL HELIUM IS QATARI AND ~1/3 OF THAT IS OFFLINE AT RAS LAFFAN, BUT KOREA'S ~6-MONTH STOCKPILES PUSH THE SEMICONDUCTOR CASCADE TO ~NOV 2026; (2) DOHA MEDIATION CONTINUES (US NOT AT THE TABLE); (3) QATAR — TOLD TO 'GO FIRST' ON THE ABRAHAM ACCORDS — STAYS CONSPICUOUSLY SILENT.** (1) **Helium [CONFIRMED, DELAYED]**: Qatar supplies ~1/3 of global helium; ~1/3 of that capacity is offline at Ras Laffan. The documented Ras Laffan→helium→semiconductor cascade is DELAYED, not absent — Korea's ~6-month stockpiles buffer it, so it re-emerges ~Nov 2026 rather than now. This is a slow-fuse supply-chain risk, not a near-term shock. (2) **Mediation [CONFIRMED, ONGOING]**: Qatar's active-mediator role (Doha talks venue) continues; the US is NOT physically at the table — Qatar is shuttling. (3) **Accords [RHETORIC — no acceptance]**: Trump again told Saudi and Qatar to 'go first'; Qatar gave NO public response and Gulf state media did NOT amplify — the absence of uptake is the slow-walk signal, consistent with Pakistan's on-the-record rejection and Saudi silence. (4) **Kuwait, not Qatar [CONTEXT]**: the day's dominant kinetic event was an IRGC calibrated ballistic missile at a US airbase in KUWAIT (intercepted, zero casualty) — not Qatar — but watch for GCC condemnation. **No Qatar-specific development resolves any thread today.**

recent_signals.headline: **QATAR'S DEAL-SHUTTLE CONCLUDED ITS DOHA ROUND — THE GHALIBAF/ARAGHCHI/HEMMATI DELEGATION (HOSTED BY QATAR'S PM) RETURNED TO TEHRAN; QATAR IS NOW AN INCREASINGLY ACTIVE MEDIATOR. TRUMP CALLS THE HORMUZ-REOPENING DEAL 'LARGELY NEGOTIATED' BUT IT STAYS UNRESOLVED ON ITS THREE HARDEST CLAUSES. QATAR — A NAMED MANDATORY-ACCORDS TARGET TOLD TO 'GO FIRST' — GAVE NO PUBLIC RESPONSE.** (1) **Doha shuttle concluded [CONFIRMED ACTION]**: the senior Iranian delegation — Speaker Ghalibaf, FM Araghchi and Central Bank governor Hemmati — concluded its Doha visit (hosted by Qatar's PM, working Hormuz + HEU + frozen-asset unfreezing) and returned to Tehran. Qatar has 'taken on an increasingly active role in advancing the negotiations' beyond a pure-messenger function; the US is NOT physically at the table — Qatar is shuttling. (2) **Deal status [CONFIRMED framework, CONTESTED contents]**: Trump says reopening Hormuz is 'largely negotiated.' Draft MOU: 60-day ceasefire extension, Hormuz reopened 'no tolls' + Iran clears its mines, US lifts the port blockade + sanctions waivers, HEU 'disposal' mechanism, 'strong inspections.' Three sticking points are explicit in US reporting: Hormuz control/status, the nuclear file, and whether the ceasefire covers Israel's Hezbollah ops (Iran insists Lebanon is covered). **The deal is simultaneously 'closer than at any point since the ceasefire' and structurally unresolved on its three hardest clauses** — and the May 25 US strikes inside southern Iran + the Lebanon escalation both cut against it. (3) **Accords target, but silent [RHETORIC]**: Trump's mandatory-Abraham-Accords demand (widened May 26 to eight nations) again told Saudi and Qatar to 'go first.' Per a US official cited by Axios the leaders — 'especially those of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Pakistan' — 'appeared stunned'; Qatar gave NO public response and Gulf state media (including Al Jazeera) conspicuously did NOT amplify the demand — the absence of uptake is itself the slow-walk intelligence. No Qatari acceptance.

Euronews May 25-27 (Doha delegation + conclusion/return to Tehran)
Trump Truth Social May 26 (Accords demand, Saudi+Qatar 'go first')

recent_signals.headline: **SENIOR IRANIAN DELEGATION ARRIVED IN DOHA MAY 25 (GHALIBAF + ARAGHCHI + CENTRAL-BANK GOVERNOR HEMMATI) TO WORK HORMUZ + HEU + FROZEN-ASSET UNFREEZING WITH QATAR'S PM — THE NEGOTIATION NOW HAS A PHYSICAL VENUE AND A MONEY TRACK. QATAR'S MEDIATOR ROLE 'INCREASINGLY ACTIVE,' BEYOND PURE MESSENGER. US NOT PHYSICALLY AT THE TABLE — QATAR SHUTTLING.** (1) **Doha delegation [CONFIRMED ACTION — Euronews, Daily Sabah, Detroit News/AP, Press TV, CNN]**: Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, FM Abbas Araghchi and Central Bank of Iran governor Abdolnaser Hemmati arrived in Doha Monday May 25 for talks focused on 'issues related to the Strait of Hormuz and highly enriched uranium' and the unfreezing of Iranian assets, meeting Qatar's Prime Minister. **The presence of the central-bank governor confirms the frozen-asset mechanics are now a live, technical negotiating workstream, not just rhetoric.** (2) **Frozen-asset money track [CONFIRMED ACTION framing per Fars]**: per Fars, 'Washington has agreed to release part of Tehran's frozen overseas assets' in exchange for lifting Hormuz restrictions and the naval blockade — Hemmati's mandate as the rial-defending CBI principal makes this testable this week. (3) **Material operational upgrade of the Qatar track**: yesterday a Qatari team was in Tehran; today Iran's economic + diplomatic principals are in Doha; Qatar has 'taken on an increasingly active role in advancing the negotiations' beyond its prior pure-messenger function. **The US is NOT physically at the table in Doha per available reporting — Qatar is shuttling.** (4) **Abraham Accords target, but silent [RHETORIC]**: Trump's Monday demand that six Muslim nations 'simultaneously' sign the Abraham Accords as part of the Iran deal — telling Saudi and Qatar to 'go first' — directly targets Doha; per Axios the leaders, 'especially those of Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Pakistan,' met it with 'silence on the line.' Gulf state media (including Al Jazeera) conspicuously did NOT amplify the demand — the absence of enthusiastic uptake is itself the intelligence that the demand is unwelcome and will be slow-walked. No Qatari acceptance.

recent_signals.headline: **AL JAZEERA (QATAR) FRAMED MUNIR-TEHRAN VISIT AS LEAD DIPLOMATIC STORY MAY 22-23. IRANIAN OFFICIAL QUOTED BY AL JAZEERA: QATAR'S ROLE IS 'VITAL, IMPORTANT, AND FUNDAMENTAL TO SUPPORTING PAKISTAN'S MEDIATION' — DUAL-CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE EXPLICIT. TTF GAS €48.69/MWh -1.46% 1D, FLAT-TO-EASING.** (1) **Munir COAS Tehran visit covered by Al Jazeera as the lead diplomatic story May 22-23** (Al Jazeera primary multi-piece); Iranian official quoted describing Qatar's role as 'vital, important, and fundamental to supporting Pakistan's mediation' — **Pakistan formal/military channel + Qatar substantive/IRGC backchannel architecture now operationally explicit in Iranian-side public framing.** (2) **Al Jazeera continued 'Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire' framing into May 23** — Qatari editorial alignment protects mediation space while channeling deal-spoiler narrative onto Israel. (3) **Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan publicly praised Trump diplomacy + Pakistan mediation** (Al Arabiya May 22-23 primary) — Gulf editorial coordination intact: Al Arabiya/Saudi = deal-text leak + deal credit; Al Jazeera/Qatar = mediation-architecture protection; The National/UAE = restraint; Oman = quiet backchannel. (4) **TTF Gas €48.69/MWh May 22 close** (-1.46% 1D, +2.1% MoM, +30%+ vs ~€31 baseline) — flat-to-easing; Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure 17% liquefaction loss persists but Norway Troll maintenance ease holding. (5) **Helium clock**: TrendForce's earlier 'two-week clock' has been operationally surpassed without confirmed Samsung/SK hynix production halt — Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final qualification with June supply ramp positioned. Korean fabs maintaining production via diversified suppliers + recycling + reduced output. **Watch trigger**: 'Samsung or SK hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage' = remains at watching despite TrendForce countdown surpassed. (6) **Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG)** still 3-5yr recovery — structural floor under European LNG pricing remains Qatar-specific. **Structural assessment**: Qatar's role in the deal architecture at maximum operational expression. The choice of Al Arabiya (Saudi) rather than Al Jazeera (Qatar) for the May 21-22 leak was MbS positioning Saudi as primary deal-credit recipient; but the Iranian-side acknowledgment of Qatar's 'vital, important, fundamental' role in the Munir visit suggests Qatar's substantive operational role is being publicly preserved.
